justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload ({"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), global Object.prototype impact explained (every object in process inherits polluted properties), safe merge fix recommended (skip __proto__/constructor/prototype keys, use schema validation, or battle-tested library), specific curl reproduction provided, safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent attack payload with curl reproduction command showing exact exploitation steps
Correctly identifies global process-wide impact — every object inherits isAdmin
Multiple fix strategies: key blocklist, schema validation with zod/joi, replacing deepMerge with shallow merge
Goes beyond minimum by catching the falsy-value bug in deepMerge and array corruption
Rollback plan includes restarting Node processes to clear polluted prototypes and auditing DB for malicious keys
Correctly notes that prototype pollution can bypass auth checks globally, matching the expected isAdmin escalation scenario

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload ({"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), global Object.prototype impact explained (affects every object in the process), safe merge fixes recommended (lodash.merge, key denylist, schema validation), safe_to_deploy is false. observations.text: Prototype pollution identified as Critical with textbook-quality explanation of the mechanism
Specific attack payload provided: {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} plus constructor.prototype variant
Global impact clearly explained: every plain object in the process inherits polluted properties
Multiple fix strategies offered: lodash.merge, key denylist, schema validation
Referenced real CVEs (CVE-2019-10744, CVE-2020-8203) for credibility
Found additional legitimate bugs (falsy-value merge, mass assignment) beyond the primary vulnerability
safe_to_deploy correctly set to false with BLOCK verdict

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload, global Object.prototype impact described, safe merge fix recommended, specific curl reproduction provided, safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Excellent explanation of the prototype pollution mechanism including process-wide impact and authentication bypass
Specific attack payload with curl command for reproduction
Multiple fix approaches: hasOwn guard, key rejection, and schema-validated allowlist
Identified additional issues beyond prototype pollution: mass assignment, falsy-default destruction, array handling
Production readiness section notes that DB corruption persists even after rollback
Correctly classified as Critical severity with BLOCK verdict
